While the benefits of AI-based tools are praised, it is important to be aware of the risks and challenges they could bring.

At the same time, when presenting this topic, teachers should find the delicate balance between awareness-raising and unnecessary details or implications that are too complex to be understood by a decade ago.
AI opens up, as well as the challenges it poses.
It is important to have an exciting conversation, open the floor to all participants and let all voices hear.
This can be done through request-based learning when students are encouraged to explore ideas, ask questions and learn through discussions.
If an AI system is trained on specific data, it can inherit biases from these data.
The quality of decisions depends on the quality of the data that is often derived from human actions.
This does not mean that the technology or algorithm is specifically discriminated against.
Rather, it means that there is a present injustice in the given territory.
We need to address this injustice before we let AI learn from these biased data and make sure we have protective measures.
It is important to take diversity into account in all aspects of cooperation with AI.
If we did not notice it from the beginning, the decisions of AI would only deepen inequality.
